name: John 5
description:
'''John William Lowery''' (born July 31, 1970), known by the stage name '''John 5''', is an American [[guitarist]]. He first took the stage name in 1998 when he left [[David Lee Roth]]'s solo band and joined [[Marilyn Manson (band)|Marilyn Manson]]. Lowery later became the guitarist for [[Rob Zombie]], and in 2022, became the touring guitarist for [[Mötley Crüe]], being promoted to a full member the following year.

He is also a solo artist, and has recorded eleven guitar albums: ''[[Vertigo (John 5 album)|Vertigo]]'' (2004), ''[[Songs for Sanity]]'' (2005), ''[[The Devil Knows My Name]]'' (2007), ''[[Requiem (John 5 album)|Requiem]]'' (2008), ''[[The Art of Malice]]'' (2010), ''[[God Told Me To (album)|God Told Me To]]'' (2012), ''[[Careful With That Axe]]'' (2014), ''Season of the Witch'' (2017), ''Invasion'' (2019), ''Sinner'' (2021), and ''Ghost'' (2025).  He also has a remix album, ''[[Remixploitation]]'' (2009), and live albums ''It's Alive'' (2018) and ''Live Invasion'' (2020). As a staff writer for [[Chrysalis Records]], he has worked with artists such as [[Avril Lavigne]], [[Rob Halford]], [[k.d. lang]], [[Garbage (band)|Garbage]], [[Meat Loaf]], [[Scorpions (band)|Scorpions]], [[Ozzy Osbourne]], [[Slash (musician)|Slash]], [[FeFe Dobson]], and [[Steve Perry]]. He has written and recorded with [[Southern rock]] band [[Lynyrd Skynyrd]].

His stage name was given to him by Manson, and refers to both [[John 5|the Bible chapter]] and him being the fifth member of Manson's band.
